title: Spin-caloritronic signatures of soft magnons in bilayer CrSBr
authors: Rob den Teuling, Ping Tang, Gerrit E. W. Bauer, Yaroslav M. Blanter
abstract: Spin transport in magnetic insulators is often treated by assuming that magnons carry a fixed spin angular momentum of $\hbar$, which does not hold in general, however. Here we calculate the magnon spin angular momentum of a layered antiferromagnet as a function of applied magnetic field and wave vector. We show that the triaxial anisotropy and intralayer dipolar interactions in bilayer CrSBr renormalize the magnon spin angular momentum, which diverges upon field-induced magnon softening. This divergence gives rise to a pronounced peak in the thermal spin Seebeck response and provides a clear spin-caloritronic signature of soft magnons.
summary: This paper demonstrates that in CrSBr, the spin angular momentum of magnons is not fixed at h-bar but diverges when magnon modes soften under an external field. This divergence creates a clear, measurable signature in the Spin Seebeck effect, providing a way to detect soft magnons via spin-caloritronic measurements.
main_problem: Investigating how non-universal magnon spin angular momentum (SAM) and field-induced magnon softening in 2D van der Waals magnets like CrSBr lead to distinct spin-caloritronic signatures.
main_result: The study finds that triaxial anisotropy and dipolar interactions cause magnon SAM to diverge during field-induced softening, resulting in a pronounced peak in the thermal and diffusive Spin Seebeck response.
method: Theoretical approach using Holstein-Primakoff transformation, Bogoliulyov transformation to diagonalize the Hamiltonian, and the linearized Boltzmann equation to calculate spin transport.
model_or_system: Monolayer and bilayer CrSBr, a van der Waals magnetic insulator with tetragonal crystal lattice, featuring intralayer ferromagnetic and interlayer antiferromagnetic coupling.
key_observables: Magnon spin angular momentum (SAM), thermal Spin Seebeck coefficient (SSC), diffusive SSC, and magnon thermal conductivity.
important_parameters: Applied magnetic field (B0), temperature (1-5 K), magnetic phases (AFM, FM, Canted, Saturated), and anisotropy axes (easy axis b, intermediate axis a).
main_assumptions: Non-interacting magnon approximation, lowest-order Holstein-Primakoff expansion, constant relaxation time approximation, and small-amplitude assumption (which breaks down near saturation).
figures_summary: Figures show the dependence of SAM, SSC, and thermal conductivity on the magnetic field for both monolayer and bilayer CrSBr, highlighting peaks and discontinuities at phase transitions and softening points.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of non-universal SAM, describes the CrSBr model and Hamiltonian, presents calculations for monolayer SAM and SSC, extends the analysis to bilayer systems, and concludes with temperature-dependent transport properties.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Calculated SAM of the Kittel mode (k = 0) in the monolayer as a function of the external magnetic field B0. The red curve shows the SAM along the easy axis ( ⃗B0 ∥ˆb) while the blue ones hold for ⃗B0 ∥ˆa in the canted (B0 < Bsat) and saturated (B0 ≥Bsat) phases: the solid (dotted) line shows the projection of the SAM along the a-axis (b-axis).

FIG. 2. SAM of the magnon modes at k = 0 in the bilayer as a function of the external magnetic field B0. The lower (higher) frequency band is indicated in blue (orange). (a) SAM along the easy axis (b) in the AFM (B0 < Bflip crit) and FM (B0 ≥Bflip crit) phases ( ⃗B0 ∥ˆb). (b) SAM along the intermediate axis (a) in the canted (B0 < Bcant sat ) and saturated (B0 ≥ Bcant sat ) phases ( ⃗B0 ∥ˆa).

FIG. 4. Diffusive SSC in a monolayer as a function of the external magnetic field B0. The transparent curves corre- spond to S(∇µ) S for all SAM equal ℏ. The red curves show S(∇µ) S along the easy axis for ⃗B0 ∥ˆb, the blue curves along the intermediate axis in the canted (B0 < Bsat) and saturated (B0 ≥Bsat) phases for ⃗B0 ∥ˆa.

FIG. 3. Thermal SSC in a monolayer as a function of the external magnetic field B0. The transparent curves corre- spond to S(∇T ) S for all SAM equal ℏ. The red curves show S(∇T ) S along the easy axis for ⃗B0 ∥ˆb, the blue curves along the intermediate axis in the canted (B0 < Bsat) and satu- rated (B0 ≥Bsat) phases for ⃗B0 ∥ˆa. The inset highlights a zoomed-in region around the peak of S(∇T ) S .

FIG. 5. Thermal SSC of the two magnon modes and their sum (black curves) in the bilayer as a function of the external magnetic field B0. The semi-transparent curves correspond to S(∇T ) S with the SAM set to ±ℏ(ℏ) in the AFM (FM) phase. (a) S(∇T ) S along the easy axis for ⃗B0 ∥ˆb in the AFM (B0 < Bflip crit) and FM (B0 ≥Bflip crit) phases. (b) S(∇T ) S along the intermediate axis for ⃗B0 ∥ˆa in the canted (B0 < Bcant sat ) and saturated (B0 ≥Bcant sat ) phases.
